title: SaveErrorDto.kind is a raw String, not a typed Rust enum — round-trip safety is asymmetric vs BlockTypeDto
description: The Sprint 8 spec REQ-IPC-010 pins `SaveErrorDto` to `{ "kind": "fs" | "validation", "reason"?: ... }`, and §15.5 explicitly upgrades `BlockType` to a typed `BlockTypeDto` Rust enum specifically because a raw `String` admits typo'd values that 'silently cross the wire and structurally typecheck on the TS side without runtime error'. The same risk applies to `SaveErrorDto.kind` and `FsErrorDto.kind`, both of which are declared as `pub kind: String` in `editor.rs:36-49`. A typo (e.g. `kind: "fs "` with a trailing space, or `kind: "validaton"`) at any compose / construction site would serialize cleanly, deserialize cleanly via `serde_json::from_str`, and only fail at the TS narrowing layer if the TS literal-union narrow rejects unknown strings (which is type-erased at runtime). PROP-IPC-019 round-trip rejection is exercised for BlockTypeDto but there is no equivalent invalid-string assertion for SaveErrorDto.kind / FsErrorDto.kind. The implementation populates these fields via string literals (`compose_state_for_save_err: SaveErrorDto { kind: "fs".to_string(), .. }` at editor.rs:273; `io_error_to_fs_kind` at editor.rs:155-164 returns one of `"permission" | "lock" | "disk-full" | "unknown"` literally) so today no typo exists, but the type system does not enforce it.
recommendation: Promote `SaveErrorDto::kind` to a typed enum (e.g. `enum SaveErrorKindDto { Fs, Validation }` with `#[serde(rename_all = "kebab-case")]`) and `FsErrorDto::kind` to a typed enum (e.g. `enum FsErrorKindDto { Permission, DiskFull, Lock, NotFound, Unknown }` with explicit kebab-case renames for `disk-full` / `not-found`). Add invalid-string PROP tests analogous to `prop_ipc_019_block_type_dto_invalid_strings`. This change is OUT-OF-SCOPE for Sprint 8 (spec §15.5 only enumerates BlockTypeDto for typed promotion); raise as a Sprint 9 candidate.
